content:
Hello! Welcome to my personal corner of the internet. I made this website to both entertain myself when I’m not busy, and to showcase my projects, photography, and publications with the world! Take a look around. I hope you enjoy!
My current projects#Enigma: The Code and the Crack55% CompleteInvestigating the history of Enigma, it's inner-workings, it's operation during World War II, and the statistical breakthroughs at Bletchley Park.
ResearchWritingDocumentationlbweb.uk WebsiteLive & DeployingA custom-built personal website using Hugo and the Blowfish theme. Automated deployment via GitHub Actions and hosted on a personal server.
HugoWebsiteHTMLCSSPhotography#Captured MomentsExploring the world through a Canon 1000D.
View Full Gallery
Hello! Welcome to my blog. Here I will hopefully keep you up-to-date on the progress regarding my projects, photography, and this website. Have fun reading!
title: Blog

content:
Hey!
Breif Overview#I’m putting all of my focus on this website and I’m hoping to publish it tonight. I’ve fixed many of the major issues I had previosuly. To list a few the about me page now has a correct background fade rather than a general blur. The photography page now has categories rather than a block of images. The photography category pages also have the image next to that images metadata. I think this looks much better compared to before. I have also created this blog section, with the intention to keep it up to date.
Image Metadata#To get the image metadata to show, I used exifr to read the metadate directly from the image bytes in the client’s browser. This is possible as I am hosting a static site where the images are placed in manually by myself, then sent to the client for both metadata reading and displaying on the page.
Favicons#I have also added new favicons to the website. To do this, I had to take a .svg file of the icon and expand it to fit a 1:1 canvas on photoshop. From here, I used favicon.io to generate all the favicon sizes compatiable with browsers across all devices.
title: Website Update 23.04.2026

content:
Hello! Based in the United Kingdom, I am a student who focuses on Science and the analysis of data. My academic background focuses on Chemistry, Physics, and Statistics, where I enjoy exploring the fundamental laws of the natural world and the data behind them.
Aside from studies, I focus my time on Photography, Research, and homelabbing. For example, I photograph the world around me using a Canon 1000D and Adobe Lightroom Classic. Take a look at some of my photographs here!
Regarding the research, I am producing research papers on things that interest me. For example, I am currently creating a research paper on the Enigma Code; specifically on its history pre-world-war, its usage in the war, how the machines work, and the bombe that was used to crack the code during the second world war. I am also looking to produce another research paper on the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) at CERN. My finished papers can be found here!
Finally, the homelabing hobby is a large and constantly growing hobby. I have a server stack which I am constantly upgrading and messing around with, to create things like this website, an XRAY VPN, a windows SMB server, etcetera. My latest project has been the creation of this website, and its hosting from my servers.
Instagram Email GitHub Discord
title: About Me

content:
<!> FORENOTE#I am currently in the process of creating this paper. You can see my progress both in the project cards on the homepage and in my blog.
Description#In this research paper, I focus in on the Enigma Code used by the Germans in World War Two. Specifically, I gloss over Enigma’s history pre-world-war-two, the inner-workings, it’s operation during the world war, and how both the English and the Polish cracked Enigma using a Bombe. All information sources are cited using Harvard’s Cite-as-you-Write, 11th edition. This has taken me over four months to complete and is the first publication I have completed.
I hope you enjoy it!
Disclaimer#This work is licensed under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. You are free to copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format, provided you give appropriate credit and do not modify the original content.
The actual document#
title: Enigma: The Code and The Crack

content: Welcome to my publications page. During my free time, I love to write research papers on subjects I enjoy. Here, you can see some of my finished papers. If you would like to see what I’m currently working on, take a look at the project cards on my homepage. Furthermore, (if I remember to) you can find a log of what I am doing to my papers in my blog.
title: Publications

content:
Welcome to my gallery. These are photographs I have taken and edited myself. I have only been a photographer for about a year and a half, and still have a lot to learn. I use a Canon 1000D, usually with a 55mm to 250mm from Canon with f/4-5.6. My photos are edited using Adobe Lightroom Classic.
My instagram for photography is @lb.photography.gb
These images are licensed under CC BY-NC-ND.
